review_text:
If I could give this park a zero I would.
The staff are EXTREMELY RUDE! They have no sense of customer service, they will more often than not greet you with a forced hello and the vibe is that dealing with customers is too much hassle for them as they sit behind their air-conditioned desk talking to each other.
The pools are rarely cleaned and the camp kitchen is disgusting with thousands of bugs in it at any one time. The kids pool area needs major repairs as there is rust and decaying wood throughout the slide park. The sewerage smell is hard to get past also and if you can make it to your site without writing your car/van off through the many major potholes then you're doing alright.
For a "family friendly" park there were more daily activity cancellations than there were running activities and when the activities did run a lot of the staff were disinterested and rude to kids.
There are plenty of other great parks in the area that provide a better customer experience/service. If you don't want to be disappointed, I'd stay clear of this already dead quiet park and find a better alternative in the areas around.
We had fun as a family and the entertainment that is all in one area is great , love the Go karts.staff are always willing to help.

Family of 7. (3 adults and 4 kids).
Park was fantastic for our 1 night stay and we are already planning a return stay.
The amenities we're a highlight for the kids and adults - jumping pad, swimming pool, splash park, mini golf and there's more onsite.
On booking, we were granted a late check out which was wonderful.

Will mention we have no park experience and did have 2 vehicles, we had to leave one outside of the boom gates close to the reception as we were advised only 1 car per site, noted for future reference.

The cabin we stayed in was quite good. The grounds were in an atrocious state. Maintenance and cleanliness were very poor. The pool area and slides are in need of an update and a clean. 2 drop slides were closed but we were not informed of that prior to arriving. Very costly experience and not worth the price.
Loving here wonderful people, school holidays over

Great place to stay. We had a short stay and appreciated the location. Close to supermarkets to get supplies and food outlets if you dont feel like cooking.
We were very impressed with the set and could t help think how much families would like the set up especially the play areas.
Amazingly professional and friendly guy greeted us after a long day of travelling. Unfortunately I dont recall his name but he is an assest to your park. The two bedroom villa was very spacious and had everything you need. Great wifi too.

Disappointed splash park not fully operational. Needed lots of work old & parts not working. Drop slides were permanently closed. Remaining slides very sticky. Mini golf needed work. Pool had bird droppings on side & in toddler end when we went for breakfast at food van at 9.30. Would have thought that would have been cleaned first thing before residents saw. Exhaust fan in cabin needed damn good clean. Positive was beds pillows were very comfortable & great air conditioning & fans in each room. Food van had great food at reasonable cost.
Good quiet place to stay with family. Away from town but close to all necessary amenities. Rooms and place was amazing and the service staff were friendly and always helpful.

We would like to start by saying the young lady who booked us in on arrival was absolutely lovely and very welcoming.

Unfortunately, that was where the positive experience ended. Upon entering our family cabin (booked for a week) - $2000 - the floors felt very dirty and we were hit with an overpowering fly spray smell. We opened all windows and turned on fans immediately, but the smell persisted. We later discovered a plug-in insect repellent device, which we turned off, however even two days later the smell remained overwhelming.

We contacted reception to see if we could upgrade to a larger cabin (happy to pay the difference), but were told nothing was available. The young man did say that the plugins were used as “pest control” but the smell has now seeped into walls and floors it seems :(

The air conditioning was also an issue — one unit was not working properly at all, and the others were not cooling effectively, which made the stay even more uncomfortable.

The grounds throughout the park appeared messy and unkept. Our biggest disappointment, however, was the pool and slides. They are very outdated, and the slides are barely functional. Many children were getting stuck halfway down and had to awkwardly shuffle their way down due to lack of water flow or poor design.

We have stayed at many BIG4 parks around Australia, and unfortunately this has been by far the most disappointing. The pricing is extremely high for what is clearly a very run-down and unattractive park. In previous years, we’ve seen this park fully booked with vans and campers, but this time it felt quite empty — and it’s not hard to see why.

Major renovations and maintenance are urgently needed.

The staff were all friendly and the park seemed okay, was a little disappointed that our site was literally right at the entrance as far as possible from the waterpark and mini golf. That all seemed like a little bit of an after thought as it was right down the back of the caravan park and when we arrived to check it out, the water was turned off so we didn't bother to go. Upon Arrival, we found our ensuite site was a tiny back to back shower and toilet block. It was a tiny space with a open shower and plastic screen. They didn't provide any floor mats or hand towels, or soap, so keep that in mind. For what we paid it was a little bit of a let down especially after coming from one of the other BIG4 parks, but perhaps we had too high of an expectation haha.

rusty and old waterpark and slides. children keeps getting stuck in the middle of the slide. spectators needs to pay full price even not going for a swim. waterpark at the strand is a lot better and it’s free, or probably go to discovery park at least it’s half the price.

We had this place booked for a few months prior to Easter weekend and we'd arrived two days before the PH. We slept the first night and our aircon turned warm. We had someone come and check it out and we were advised that changing the remote may work. If that didn't work, they would get an electrician in. The next night, it had turned warm again and we had let the reception know about it first thing in the morning. The groundsman was sent to check out our aircon and we were told that they had changed the motor out a few weeks prior and they've had nothing but trouble with it. (Yet they still put someone in the cabin). We went down for breakfast at the van and then back to reception to see what they worked out. The reception had told us that there wouldn't be an electrician available on good friday and they cannot put us anywhere else. I had asked them what we'll do if it turns warm again. And she had said there's nothing we can do because there are no rooms available. We were charged free the first night and 50% off our second night and a refund for rest and we are now staying with family. Pretty poor considering its Easter Weekend with our kids and we booked months in advance, yet they still put people in rooms that they know they're having trouble with. Two devastated kids that cant enjoy a family vacation on holidays.

This place is average to say the least. Definitely wouldn’t rush there with your family. Splash park hardly working full
Of black mould. Two slide connected to the main pool don’t have enough water running making them a bit of a hazard.  Dirty nappies and rubbish everywhere We ended up leaving and not staying the night as there were lots of questionable male residents making our stay also feel unsafe
We loved our stay! Staff were so freindly, and the park had amazing features. We will return!

Thank you to BIG4 Ingenia Holidays Townsville in North Queensland for a comfortable overnight stay at the end of February.

Nan and I had just flown in from Kuala Lumpur to spend a couple of weeks holiday with Dennis around Townsville and Ingham. Dennis had been staying there with his campervan for the past fortnight and booked a simple cabin for the three of us for the night.

We arrived after a stormy travel day flying from Sydney to Townsville with Qantas. The flight was delayed due to continuous rain, but the pilot and crew handled the conditions well and got us there safely.

After the long day of travel, it was nice to have a clean cabin, a comfortable bed, good shower facilities, and a small kitchenette with tea and coffee. Just what was needed for a good night’s rest before starting our time in Queensland. We even managed to dry out damp cloths from the flight at the holiday park laundry as it was raining heavily and the luggage got slightly wet from the baggage transfer from airport luggage transporter to aeroplane at Sydney earlier in the day during flight.

Thanks again for the stay. 🌿
The park is a bit tired

We stayed 11 nights here in a 2bdm villa. The villa had a weird smell when we arrived so I did another proper clean. There was an ant infestation in the kitchen on the bench coming from the window. I let reception know and a guy came and had a look around and just said ‘do you know where they’re coming from? Because I need to know where they’re coming from to put bait in’ and that was it. Nothing done there so I just had to keep spraying Mortein everyday. The grass area near the big pool stunk so bad everytime we walk through to the pool. The big pool itself needed a good clean. If you like swimming with dead bugs and cockroaches go ahead. The waterslide is shocking. Every single person gets stuck in the middle because you just come to a halt. They definitely need to look into replacing that whole slide. The 2 long slides that were Permanently Closed looked much better than the small ones. Didn’t even know they were closed would’ve been good to note that since we had our older kids with us. And don’t even go there with the camp kitchens. The one near the big pool had a kitchen area that was full of dust and cobwebs and just neglected. Hadn’t been cleaned in over a year for sure. Luckily the BBQ plates were clean but that was it. The other Camp Kitchen next to the laundry  appeared better at first. Looks clean but then we went to cook our kids party pies in the oven and oh my goodness! It was so dirty in that oven you wouldn’t even want to cook a single thing in there. The oven looked like it hadn’t been cleaned for years! No exaggeration! It was absolutely filthy and disgusting. But the most disappointing thing was the ladies at the front reception. They just seemed like they had a chip on their shoulder and speak to you like you’re dumb. And Jenna has no smile for anyone. The other lady who I didn’t bother to get her name just had an attitude like you’re taking up too much of her time. If you have that kind of attitude how’s about finding other employment? The only nice and polite person at the front was the younger girl. I actually felt sorry for her to have to work with these women. We’ve stayed at a few Ingenia Holiday parks in NSW and I gotta say this was the worst we’ve been to. This was our second time to Townsville due to visiting our sick brother. We will never stay here again. Our first time in Townsville we stayed at Discovery Parks Townsville in Wulguru and that was 10/10 in regard to cleanliness and service. Unfortunately they weren’t available for us this time so we stayed here due to our large group. What a mistake that was and never again. My other kids ended up staying with us here but had originally booked at Discovery Parks Townsville. They really should’ve stayed over there. But anyhow that was our experience at this place.

We arrived after hours, and had been sent details of how to access our key, which all went ok. The boom gate code didn't work, and we had to ring to get a new code, which took an extra 5 mins - not too big of a deal. The cabin had a sewage smell, which I think may have come from the toilet not being sealed properly. The playground, waterpark and slides all need desperate attention. Unfortunately, I did not see the large slides were out of action permanently until I got an update via email. The small slides - the kids could not slide down, as they are worn out, and many kids just walked down. Overall the site was average at best. There is patchy mobile coverage, which can't be helped by the site - wifi was ok in most places.
Ensuit site was amazing.  All facilities were first class as were the staff and service.  100% will book in there again if im ever back in Townsville

Overall, this park is average compared to other Big 4 parks. The biggest issue for me was the water park. It is in desperate need of refurbishment with the drop slide permanently closed. The water slides in the main pool also seem unsafe. The water flow is poor, resulting in kids getting stuck halfway down. I saw several children stand up on the slides trying to manoeuvre themselves further down...which is a major safety concern—one slip could easily result in a fall of around 2 metres. I believe this issue should be properly fixed or the slides should be closed.

A minor issue was reception and connectivity. Mobile reception around the park was poor and the Wi-Fi was unreliable.

Arrived after hours and called the number provided. The staff member who came to the office was amazing!
We asked for a powered site to park our van on and were given a ensuite site for the same price. Everything was super clean, plenty of staff running around looking after the place, it seemed like it focused on tourists rather than emergency accommodation, which was nice. This is our first trip without our kids but I’m sure they would have loved this park with the splash park and big water slides!
We highly recommend this park if you are visiting Townsville.
Nice cabins, very clean.
Kids want to stay here for the fun and activities but the best slides were closed, the one running barely wet and activities cancelled the same day. Doesn't compare to pics online

Clean and super spacious cabins, facilities well kept and great place overall for a family.
Staff at the reception was super friendly and went out of their way to accommodate our early arrival.
Thank you!
Recommended and will come back.
The whole family loved everything. With all the amenities kept them busy all day. The cabin had everything you wanted even the air-conditioning was put on before arriving.

Great park only downfall is there is a huge kids splash park and pool with slide a great area but in the tranquil pool the other end of the park you’ve also got kids screaming and splashing. This should be an adults only pool. This park caters for kids and has forgotten about adults not travelling with kids.
Ensuite site was great value and the ensuite was freshly renovated and clean, but very hot with little to no airflow.

Beautiful caravan park. We paid $42 for a powered drive through powered site. I booked over the phone on our way, and was sent all details via email immediately, to allow a smooth after-hours check in. We enjoyed the pool in the morning before we left, but didn't have time to play mini golf, although saw others enjoying the course. The bbq and camp kitchen area was clean and tidy, with everything we needed.

Half of the shower block was closed as they were replacing hot water systems on the other half. Unfortunately, the chippy didn't check to make sure no one was using the showers before turning off the water. After walking out in a towel, covered in shampoo and soap suds, I laughed it off after scaring one of the other workers to ask how long it would be off. The shower amenities themselves are great. They ensuite style layout is really good, with plenty of space and everything you need, cleaned and well maintained.
Kids friendly

Really cool Big4 caravan park just outsideTownsville.
Great amenities and so much for the kids!
Clean facilities friendly and efficient service
All the outdoor staff never smiled or said hello to us, so rude!! The girls at reception were all lovely
Fabulous caravan park, just north of the city. Excellent facilities including ensuite sites and amazing pool, playground and mini golf ⛳️
We stayed on a powered,  ensuite site for $45! Amazing price. Very nice camp kitchen, laundry and pool.

Unfortunately not the best experience, the floor of our cabin was very dirty, basic services such as being able to log into your account on Netflix isn’t available on the tv, we were told to mirror it from our phone this also didn’t work, management were not very helpful. Don’t stay down the bottom of the park, the dump station for the caravan absolutely stinks, so much so we left early because we couldn’t eat our breakfast.
Great facilities. Cafe on site and staff were very friendly and helpful. Located on a busy road and near railway line so can be noisy.
A little isolated between main roads. No walking tracks anywhere near.

The park is huge with spacey powered sites, the staff are very friendly and helpful. The park has lots to offer. Swimming pools, mini putt putt, water parks with slides, outdoor cinema. The only issue I had was being placed near the septic outlets. I highly recommend you look into this outlet's and not placing anyone near them. They are a little smelly. But as a park, it was well maintained. I do recommend, just ask not to be near the smelly outlets.
Excellent facilities.   Well equipped cabins.   Friendly staff.

Had stayed at this Park on 3 previous occasions but this last stay will be our last. Ensuite site and ensuite was rundown and had odours. Also requested a previous site we had stayed on and told it was already booked We were there 3 weeks and no one used the requested site. Very disappointing
Excellent park, great sites , great service.  Definitely recommend. We will stay again

The staff were absolutely lovely and so accomodating for a last minute booking. The park is so well equipped for adults and children, and there is so much to do. All the facilities were clean and well maintained with everything you need! Would come back and recommend
Nous avons séjourné deux nuits avec notre van, le camping est sympathique mais l’odeur de la fausse sceptique est vraiment désagréable ! Il faut avoir un emplacement loin des toilettes… dommage

This place is good for the young families with lots of activities for them. It does need a little bit of maintenance or refurbishment to some areas of the golf course, the small water slides. There is not a lot for the older children, with the big water slide closed. Yes the new jumping bed is safer than the jumping pillow. The new villas look interesting.

THE SMELL. if you stay make sure you pick a site near the front of the park because down the back it smells like a septic tank, constantly. water park is not well maintained and full of rust and moss that could be pressure washed off. pool is clean and a nice spot for the kids. Sites are dusty with a small amount of grass. didn't find the park noisy at all considering it was between 2 freeways. It's in a good location and the staff are lovely. We stayed an extra night simply because we weren't done in the area and didn't want to go to another park. Would probably choose to stay elsewhere next time.

We just returned from holding our school camp and staying at Big 4 Ingenia, Deeragun. Our accommodation was well-suited to hosting 10/11 year old children, with the large swimming pool and slides being particularly popular. I would also like to give a shout-out to our host, Jody, who was fantastic. She cooked us delicious meals and made excellent packed lunches. She also did a great job assisting us throughout the week and answering our numerous questions. I would definitely recommend Jody and Big 4 Ingenia, Deeragun for all future camping/cabin accommodation in the Townsville area.
Fantastic friendly staff and super helpful. Very clean park and fantastic cabins to stay in.
It gives a larger than life impression!
Great little  Park  friendly staff

We spent 5 days at Big4 Ingenia.
Even though it was the 1st week of school holidays, it wasn't packed, which was awesome. We stayed in a 3 bedroom villa which was wonderful. The whole unit was A/C. There was plenty of room and a nice kitcenette. The park was well maintained, clean, and quiet. Kids loved the pool, slides, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal carts and each arvo/ night they had other activities like Lazer tag, archery, movies and Nintendo nights for the kiddies.
It is a little way out of town, 10-15 min drive to the Strand, but there are 2 close by shopping precincts with everything you need.
We will definitely be going back 👍
Large caravan park, huge sites easy access. Close to all facilities.
About 20 minutes out of Townsville, very quiet
Plenty of room for the van and car, quiet park, great for kids etc

Excellent caravan park.
Heaps for kids to do.
Quiet areas of adults.

So this park is the epitome of the saying that the site you get makes all the difference. We came here twice, the first time we were placed on the very first site you come to when entering the park. It is close to the highway, the train track, a small pool directly opposite, and worse, every single car and van that comes in the park drove right by our site. Some might like that but we didn’t. We had already booked our return stay but if we hadn’t we would have gone somewhere else. We are so glad we didn’t. Before we left the first time we enquired about a different site and the staff were more than happy to oblige. On our return we were in a site we picked, further into the park, and it was awesome. It was an ensuite site (most are at this park), and being more central it wasn’t a hike to get to the big pool, laundry, or cafe. We are here now for 11 days, and if we come back we would stay here again.

It is about 15kms away from the Strand/townsville CBD, but there is a bunch of shops about two minutes drive away. A Cole’s, fast food places, some takeaway restaurants, super cheap auto, and BCF. About ten-15 minutes away are the huge malls that have everything. Close enough not to have to take a packed lunch, but far enough away you don’t hear much about shenanigans going on in the park. Great for kids (they have different activities every day, including laser tag!!!), but peaceful for the adults too. To quote a movie, how’s the serenity! The staff are great and keep the park looking great.

Sorry, ingenia's are usually amazing, but this isn't to the same standard. Sewerage smell was horrid. Short stays placed amongst Too many run down long terms didn't leave a good impression. Going back to our favourite holiday park. Backpackers taking over site and reception rude. Won't be back

Staff friendly and sorted out the incorrect outstanding fee as we had paid in full. Camp kitchen clean. Filthy amenities. It had rained, so we gave them the benefit of the doubt and used our caravan facilities. Long term neglect with house spiders and insects encrusted on window sills, light fittings, toilet paper dispenser etc. Dirt, hair and dead bugs on the floors. Went to use the mop but the water and mop itself were too filthy to make much of a difference. Top pool needed cleaning and scooping of litter off the bottom, but was undergoing maintenance the day we left. Disappointed is an under statement.

I thoroughly enjoyed staying here. The staff were amazing. They gave us regular updates about our room and services. You can just tell when management like there job and treat there customers as such. I would recommend this place to everyone. What I liked was
✅the garden in front of our balcony 🌺, nice view and privacy
✅ Great communication from staff
✅ the shady umbrellas and seats by the pool
✅Price, the price made it an affordable holiday for me and my baby.
Things I did not like,
Is that the lights in the kitchen were like spot lights SOO bright I could not turn them on, like why on earth would anyone want it so bright!? It is a 10 min drive to the city centre but the traffic flows well.
Thanks for a great holiday.

The ensuite had a lot of mould on the inside of the door.
The location is very well looked after. The gardens and pools are lovely. The area is noisy because of the road traffic

We had such a great time here! We only wish we'd stayed longer. We had an issue with the initial room, but the night manager was all over it, and sorted us out pronto. Such great service. Cleanliness was too notch, facilities were awesome. Our kids had the best time at the splash park, the playground and the pool. Highly recommended! Easy to find, right next to the highway but low noise. Clear check-in instructions and highly responsive communication We'll definitely be back here, loved it.
Wonderful place to stay in Townsville plenty to do great amenities

We stayed one night , the park facilities are very kid friendly , great pool and jumping pillow. Grounds could be kept neater , lots of overgrown grass especially during school holiday time.
The kids wanted to hire out the bike carts and the kids were trying out which bike suited their height before we purchased the hire , the young girl came out and told us to get the kids off them before we payed for hire ! When o explained they needed to see what one suited their size she was still very rude , arrogant and down right aggressive ! It really put a sour Taste in our mouths but we wanted the kids to have fun so payed for the hire for the hour !
After that we would never go back !
We have never come across staff in a holiday park or hotel ever speak to rudely to guests !

The bathrooms are in need of a big clean and renovation.  Very disappointed that the 2 slides into the main pool can not be used if you are over the age of 13. Most staff are very friendly.  The big slides are good to see at a carvan park.
Day passes = so much fun. Had my son’s birthday party there. So much for the kids to do. Facilities were nice and clean. A really great day 😃

I wish i could leave zero stars if i could.

We recently stayed at this Big 4 caravan park in Townsville and were extremely disappointed. Compared to other Big 4 parks we’ve visited, this one fell well below expectations. The entire park felt old, outdated, and poorly maintained, with little to no visible upkeep.

The campsites were dirty and overgrown, and the public amenities block near the pool was filthy—covered in mould, with dirty walls, doors, and floors. Honestly, public toilets on highways are cleaner. It was clear they hadn’t been cleaned in days. The overall cleanliness of the park was appalling, with overgrown grass, unkempt grounds, and a general lack of care from management.

Our kids couldn’t use the jumping pillow because it was covered in mould. The mini-golf course was completely overgrown and muddy. There were hardly any proper paths, so we had to trudge through mud just to get anywhere in the park. Despite no rain for three days, drainage was clearly an issue, with stagnant, green, mouldy water sitting in puddles on roads and footpaths. With diseases in the ground in and around townsville at the moment and some people dying from this. having this stagnent water and mud just sitting there everywhere for all the guest to walk through was appaling.  It was dangerously slippery, and both adults and kids were at risk of falling. It was obvious this wasn’t a new problem, yet no effort had been made to address it.

We paid a significant amount for a two-night stay in a three-bedroom cabin. While it was clean and looked nice, it was small and cramped—not worth the price. Some of the other cabins near the entrance were old, rundown, and in desperate need of replacement.

As frequent Big 4 guests and current members, we were incredibly disappointed. This park does not reflect the high standards we expect from the Big 4 brand. In fact, it shouldn’t even be carrying the Big 4 name.

We would not recommend this park to anyone and will not be returning.

Great caravan park on the outskirts of Townsville. 10 minute drive back into the city but also has shops close by for everyday items. Great facilities with immaculate waterpark, splash park, water slides and jumping pillow. Plenty of activities to keep the kids amused. Various accommodation options from cabins and villas to powered, ensuite and unpowered sites.

Stayed 1 night in a cabin for a work trip.

Perfect location for us, short drive to shops, takeaways and petrol station.

Cabin was fresh, clean and well maintained with lovely shampoo, conditioner and body wash provided as well as water already chilled in the fridge on arrival.

The beds were comfy, air con worked well and the furniture was of good quality.

The grounds are very well kept, clean with well maintained grass and pool area.

We didn't venture into the pool.due to having no time, but it looked great.

The lady on reception when we checked in was really lovely and friendly.

I have nothing negative to say about this place.

We stayed here for 3 nights in January, in a 2 bedroom villa. The accommodation was great - some minor maintenance issues - but comfortable and tidy. The customer service was exceptional. Unfortunately the slides, splash pad and other kids amenities were disappointing and very run down. They no longer look like the pictures on the website. Given that we booked this accommodation for the kids amenities, we were let down. The slides in the main pool were not slippery and incredibly slow - kids were coming to a stop in the middle. And the slides structure and splash pad were very weathered, mouldy and in poor condition. :-( Unfortunately it rained the entire time we were there, so we couldn't use the bouncing pillow, but that area also looked unenticing.

Absolutely loved our quick stay! The staff were very friendly; the cabins were very clean and well-equipped with everything we needed. The kids loved the pool and slides. It is very well-priced, and I would definitely recommend it to families going on holiday.
Beautiful and luxurious brand new 3 bedroon cabins. Loved it!

Very happy with our stay! The staff were fantastic right from the get go, the rooms were lovely and clean. We stayed in a 2bdr villa and was one of the nicer ones we’ve stayed in. Lots of kids activities and awesome pools and slides! Pools were a bit dirty but with the sheer amount of kids going in and out I’m not surprised.
The kids were very upset when we had to leave. I think that’s a good indication that they loved their stay too.

Fabulous stay at Big 4 Ingenia Holidays Townsville. Our room was roomy, clean and freezing cold on arrival (a lovely touch considering how hot it was outside). The water slides and pools were fantastic, lots of great facilities. Great location, will definitely be back and highly recommend to travelers especially those with kids. Great value for money.

A nice, quiet park. Roads were not the best, but they put us in a site right across from the smaller pool. The pool wasn’t too good, as it was quite hot, so we went to the other pool with the slides. It was even hotter, but the drop slides were so fun. The other two children slides weren’t the best. The kids kept on getting stuck. Nice barbecue and ensuite. Maybe just add some hand soap in the ensuites!

The only reason I give two stars is because the water park and pool were absolutely amazing. We arrived at 9:40am after a long 9 hour drive with a baby and toddler upon arrival they told us to come back at 2pm as that was there arrival time but no where on the emails or text messages did it inform us of that but fair enough we respected that. Staff was kinda rude but when informed of our long drive did agree to text us when our Villa was ready. After checking in we came to find the place smelt kinda funky and was very small and outdated not happy with the amount we paid, the kitchen was tiny, bathroom had one small window and just smelted terrible the entire stay, the main bedroom bed kept moving around and didn’t have any stoppers, the mattress would move off the base though the night and the blinds on the windows did nothing to keep the sun out wasn’t ideal for putting young ones down for naps during the day and the pillows were cheap and uncomfortable. The second bedroom (kids room) were definitely not cleaned to standard as our two year old found not one but two old dummies under the roll out bed and proceeded to put them in her mouth. YUCK! Found on our last day we were relaxing on the couch only be covered in bites and itchy not sure what was going on there but someone needs to check for bugs. Our family travels a lot and staying at Big 4s has been a big part of that but this one doesn’t live up to the name…. Needless to say we won’t be visiting this one again.
Facilities were pretty good. Kitchen and bathrooms were clean. The thing we didn't like was all the noise throughout the night from trucks on the nearby busy highway
Beautiful and tidy accommodation with great staff
Camping sympa et idéalement situé près de la ville et des axes routiers. Les parties communes sont propres et bien équipées. Prix attractif. Les emplacements sont grand et très bien
Very good for the children in the water park. Jumping pillow. Friendly ground staff. Would use this rv park again.

We hv caravan so were camping
Pool is good and  showers clean

Kids loved the waterpark, drop slide, pool, hire bikes and  jumping pillow. Basketball court was good just a little high for my little one.
Accomodation was very nice had everything we needed, just the fresh paint smell when we arrived was a bit overwhelming. We put fans on and aired it out and was good the following day.

Hmmmmm, the room was old outdated and very basic. Very expensive for what was offered. However! It seems the park is under construction and the new area looks amazing with brand new cabins splash park and playground. A visit in another 12 months would probably be completely different. The old pool area is lovely and the staff are friendly and efficient.

Friendly helpful staff . Condition of cabin was disappointing. Decorative cushion on bed soiled , blinds not fitting windows soiled as well . Ensuite door dirty  not closing properly. Sliding entry door difficult to slide .
Not a good standard for a Deluxe Villa.
Poor areas awesome.

We loved our stay at Ingenia!!
We have stayed here in the past, in our caravan, but this time for our daughter’s 6th Birthday we decided to get a 2bdr ‘Villa’. We love this park, we really do! We love the activities for the kids, we love how friendly it is, we love that the incredible staff go above & beyond (our son threw up everywhere the morning of us checking out & the receptionist was incredible, she extended our check out until 12noon so I could clean up without rushing!) & we were so grateful! This is the first time we’ve had any kind of issues so I feel sad writing this next bit… but… the issues were as follows 😔
Cleanliness: wasn’t great at all in our Villa - dust, dirt, hair ect built up in corners, on window sills, skirting boards ect…
There was some mould around the washing machine & in the shower which would easily be fixed with a decent scrub & some good chemicals.
The sliding door runners were filthy, really not nice & gross. Filled with mouse poo, leaves, all manner of things…
The walls were quite dirty, they haven’t been cleaned in a while as I got a cloth to see if just water would get some of the dirt off & it did. The walls, doors, door frames & behind the washing machine all need a very good wipe over! It’s never nice walking into a place & visibly seeing other peoples dirty marks everywhere & hand prints/foot prints.
Sun Control Indoors: The black out blinds definitely don’t black out much sadly. Especially the main bedroom where the sun shines directly on the window for a large portion of the afternoon (naturally that’s usually the time you want to relax most haha).
Privacy & Sun invasion: Sitting on the patio in the mornings was impossible with the sun streaming straight in over the entire area, making everything quite hot to touch & just not a nice place (not to mention warm) to sit especially if you are an early riser & the type that likes to sit with a morning cuppa & a book for a few hours before the kids get up. Also at night the lights from the toilet block are blinding… all night, so, with both of those lighting issues, it would be brilliant to put up an outdoor blind or shade cloth to combat that. It would greatly improve the feeling of privacy too!
Size: The rooms are VERY small. We had a hole in our cupboard from the suitcase stand because that’s obviously where it lives as there is no room for it elsewhere in the room.
The second bedroom with the 2 sets of bunks had zero room, there is no cupboard at all but there is a bedside table & the gap between bunks is quite narrow.
Likes: We LOVED that the WIFI was SUPER fast!! Because the signal isn’t great at the park it was awesome having the WIFI. We had several devices & both smart TVs connected to it & it ran so well! We were shocked because cause it’s without a doubt the best WIFI we’ve experienced at any park, hotel, motel ect…
We really like that the main TV was of a large size & also a Smart Tv with all the apps! I actually did an update on both TVs so there are now more apps & things to choose from which was fantastic!
The TV in the main bedroom was also amazing, large in size & a Smart TV so had there have been black out blinds, it would have been a relaxation haven! So overall for the price of over $200 per night, it wasn’t really worth it for the quality, cleanliness & size BUT we will absolutely be back because we love it more than any of the other parks in Townsville & the fun our kids have out weighs the issues for us. Not to say we don’t hope they rectify some of them & even better, before our next stay :)
My name is luke Zappala, our family enjoyed staying long weekend at big 4 Ingenia we will be back after Christmas great service.

Grounds were nice and clean. Amenities block was clean, good showers, hot water. Pool was very nice, but the kids slides into the pool need attention, every kid has to push themselves the whole way down the slide.

Van spots where dusty and no grass
I get it if no rain and water restrictions but we had a huge mess to clean up once we left as it was impossible to keep the dirt out of the van and beds, compared to other Ingenia big 4s we’ve stayed at it just felt a bit like the grounds weren’t a priority

Check in was rushed and a little dismissive of some questions about the online check in messages etc in the end I don’t really care but it was definitely a different vibe to everywhere else  we’ve stayed at in nth/ tropical nth invenias

The big slides where a huge hit for the kids and they get all three of our stars
Older kids spent the whole time on the bigger slides with a lot of fun had and the staff manning the big slide was lovely and kind!

Met lots of other friendly travellers too!

Stayed 5 nights in a villa with my daughter.
So much to do, pool and splash park are awesome :)
Staff were friendly and very helpful
The park has a beautiful waterpark but the Amenities are old and need renovation.
Staff and and service are fantastic but I’m guessing it was there long before the by-pass and the noise all night long was insane sorry 😩
Excellent park, the facilities are clean and the staff are amazing and super friendly. Sites are a decent size. Shopping centres close by, highly recommend.
Nice clean park with excellent amenities.

Time to get serious. You want a place with great staff, clean, spacious site, great amenities, good no further. This place was the absolute perfect site for a feet up and a bundy.|Giant chess set, great pools and water park even mini golf.||FANTASTIC
Everything spotlessly clean, staff awesome 👌

Lovely people who went out of their way to accommodate us and our pooches , amenities are clean tidy and neat shower is hot with good pressure lots of things to do here ,close to super market and bushland beach(dog friendly) .
Location is between two major roads however we knew this when we booked so not a problem ,as I said before good people who greet you with a smile at the gate and go out of their way to help.

Nice setting, well out of town. Staff were rude and curt. Amenities are quite run down... serious crack in shower floors, rarely clean and the septic system stank, which sucked camping in a site right next to the toilet block. The place looks like it is almost ready to fall over. Not nearly worth it for the money.

close to shops for groceries and supplies, the park was a delight for kids and families. Lots of fun activities and on-site amenities, water park, pools, putt putt golf, scooters and carts to ride. Nice and quiet experience for my wife to enjoy with the family and grandkids. Highly recommended 👌.
We found the park to be well situated,very friendly and helpfull staff, we loved the quietness of the park and the friendly people staying their
Very smelly site, included septic pit which stunk.! Nothing done by management to solve the horrible problem.

We stayed on Mon 27 May 2024, 1 night.|We only stayed one night, as we were travelling north.|The Park is conveniently located alongside the main highway.|There are two highways and a railway line passing by, but we did not have too much noise, thank goodness, so all OK.|The cabins are modest, but clean.|The Manager was helpful as to where to get meals in Townsville.|Townsville has a great esplanade to walk.|The Park is recommended.

We stayed on Mon 27 May 2024, 1 night.
We only stayed one night, as we were travelling north.
The Park is conveniently located alongside the main highway.
There are two highways and a railway line passing by, but we did not have too much noise, thank goodness, so all OK.
The cabins are modest, but clean.
The Manager was helpful as to where to get meals in Townsville.
Townsville has a great esplanade to walk.
The Park is recommended.
Location is ok but have a smell of sewerage all night hence the rating.

Had a weekend here with our daughters family who have van, three girls under 10 years and they had so much fun , the amenities for families are superb kept them going all afternoon. The snack shack was also a great treat for and icecream and hot chips 😋

This cabin was a big letdown after such a lovely one in Cairns - it gave the air of a very amateur handyman having tried to do some maintenance and it was very rough indeed.  Brand new shower and a mirror which covered half the window which was strange.  Lower bunk window boarded up, no locks on the windows, not well maintained at all.  It looked like neither the main bed nor the bunks had been made properly, the valance not straightened on to the base, the bed pane wrinkled and unironed - not an attractive site.  The front curtain across the door didn't fit and was hanging on one bracket and the blinds on the windows looked like they've been cut from a bigger one with fraying edges and wobbly on the hinges.  This cabin was classed as 'deluxe ensuite' - didn't live up to its name.  All in all, we would never recommend this park at all.

A nice park with great amenities for kids.
We ate at the onsite Cafe and the food was very good and a good size.
Amenities were clean and well maintained.

Wonderful service at reception. The play area is also amazing, it's kids heaven! Two swimming pools, water slides, playground and a jumping pillow. Make sure you check the park map before you book to make sure you're in an area that's right for you - we booked an ensuite suite but unfortunately they are located too far away from the kids activities for us. We elected to move to a grass powered site.

The location is right on the highway, so you can't walk anywhere, but the city isn't too far away by car.

Had a caravan site close to dump point and sewerage plant, could smell sewerage most of the time.  The park was nearly empty so we could have been put further away.  Other than that we like this park and have stayed many times.  Large site plenty of room for annex and car.  Clean and tidy grounds.

First sites couldn't get into as 4 kids toys cars etc on the sites, told reception went to another site and tge next day they locked us in with kids & dogs all around us. Plenty of empty sites but placed everyone on top of each other. Bathrooms dirty & in poor condition. Left 3 days early
We only stayed one night but it was great

Such a shame this park is open for public day use. We stayed two nights, the first day the facilities were only used by park patrons but then on Anzac Day and the day after it felt like we were at a public pool/ park with lots of groups of people around having gatherings. Gave the park a completely different vibe and made the facilities very crowded. People had setup picnic areas near the pools etc. Also didn’t make us feel very secure having our van sit there with visitors cars driving past and visitors walking all around the park past/through sites. Due to that reason alone we will find a park that is soely run for park guest and their visiting families only.
What a great place to stay in Townsville. Lots of things to do for the kids on the school holidays. Comfortable cabins. Wonderful customer service.
I can't recommend this place. Our cabin wasn't cleaned properly and there was an overwhelming stench of sewerage across the whole place. I could go on but I can't be bothered.

Very busy as you would expect for a Big 4. Ladies at reception were LOVELY! The water slides and pool are great, and the little kiosk area that is open on a Friday and Saturday evening was reasonably priced and tasty.  Didn't try the mini golf, but the playground and jumping pillow areas needed a little TLC. Work needs to be done to the access to the pool too for the wet season so you don't have to walk through mud to get to the play and pool areas, it was a bit gross and smelly and a mozzie minefield.

Wouldn't stay anywhere else in Townsville, this is too good.

My experience started with friendly service with Jemma at the front desk and ended with a smile with Jody the park manager. I was solo travelling for work, but this would be a great park to keep the kids entertained with splashpark, 6m waterslides, mini golf and a pumping kids club.

My cabin was good - clean and just what I needed. It looks like they just put in some brand new cabins which I will book next time.

An sich ganz in Ordnung.
Man hat einen Pool und viele Spielmöglichkeiten für die kleinen. Allerdings ist alles merklich in die Jahre gekommen und nicht mehr so gut in Schuss.
Die BBQs, Badezimmer und Küche sind sehr sauber und wirken gut instandgehalten.

Ansonsten ist es eher leider eher langweilig und es ist kaum Besuch hier. (Mindestens der halbe Park war komplett verwaist)

Für einen Kurzen Aufenthalt top, aber wird eben schnell langweilig.

Seems like a great place haven't stayed the night there.. was going to come use the water park for a play with the little one yet there price is $20 pp the crazy when you can go anywhere else and it's 6$ or Goto sugar World and it's only $14 pp wouldn't recommend going to the big 4 till the drop there price on being able Togo for a swim.. the price just for a swim is rediculusly crazy only wanted Togo for a swim not stay the night or anything and they wanted $40 for me to take my 17 month old for a swim and wanted to charge $20 for the misso to be able to just sit and watch...

Room was run down, play grounds filthy and require maintenance.
Thick slime around kids water park. Park was riddled with mozzies.

$20 per person just to enter the park for a day regardless of swimming or not. We're locals which I mentioned several times, we had a large group of people meet for my son's 7th birthday, wasn't until most people paid when someone said do we get a local discount then she applied it to theirs, so they paid $60 for 4 people which was still way too much. Considering we've been 3 times before and not charged for adults not swimming, and then this time the kids were told they can't use the slides even though they were 5 years+ like the sign said, and had used it during our 3 other trips. They other side's were half dry and kids couldn't even slide on them properly. Pool was warm, and little shade around it. We all left feeling like it was a big money grab considering what they got for it. We will drive the extra 10 minutes to North Shore and pay $4 each for the same experience. Left a bad taste in a lot of locals mouths. We won't be returning unfortunately.

Very excited and had high expectations after previously visiting a Big 4 Ingenia in Cairns. Unfortunately Townsville falls well short as some basic fundamentals of living in the tropics are lacking.
1/ Shade. The main pool had no shade over it which means in the hotter months its like getting into a warm bath. Very unpleasant.  The kids only lasted 20 minutes before they bailed, both looked like lobsters.
2/Sensible Timing of outdoor activities- Though the pool didn't get any cooler after dark, it would have been nice to use some of the other facilities in the cool of the day which obviously occurs later in summer. To close things off at 6pm or even 4pm! on our long hot days feels like being short-changed.
This is day one of three and frankly I'm not looking forward to the next few days trying to keep the kids cool and entertained. If I hadn't paid so much for the stay, at this point I would gladly opt for a hotel with a cool pool that was usable.
On the upside the well appointed cabins had awesome air-conditioning and were in a rare shady spot.
Also the staff were lovely and accommodating..

This is a nice caravan park, with amenities such as a pool, showers, laundry (with several clothes lines), and a kitchen.

The showers and bathrooms are a bit grubby. It seems like they may only get cleaned once per day.

On the other hand, the kitchen was spotless. I didn't use the pool or laundry.

The location is a bit of a ways out of town, and twice overnight I heard loud trains passing nearby. I was camping in a tent, so perhaps they wouldn't be as noticeable if I had stayed in a cabin or caravan instead.

Tired and terribly located. Does not look like the photos anymore, and is located between multiple highways, 20 minutes from town. My impression was someone once spent a lot of money building this, and it's not paid off, so it's not maintained or loved at all. Waterslides had a flat spot in the middle so didn't work properly, landscaping has fallen apart and sites are mostly gravel.

Would not recommend.
Rooms are great, location is fantastic, the receptionist was so rude and unhelpful, would never stay here again because of her

We had a nice stay at this park. The amenities were very clean and so was the camp kitchen, staff was very friendly and helpful, this is a strong feature of this park. We travel with a small campervan which made out camp spot humongous :)  There is road noise but that didn't keep us awake. The stone curlews made some noise but they are funny birds, so yeah.

The cabin was amazing 😍 definitely exceeded our expectations. Will definitely be back!!
Kids loved the pool and entertainment. Close enough to shops and take away places for a quick shop.
Great place to stay and the children have so much fun
My husband and I bought food off the food van and spent the night and on our way home with chronic  stomach problems. Avoid them if you don’t want to live on the toilet

Great park, especially if you have kids. A huge resort pool with slides plus another smaller pool which would probably have fewer kids in it. Splash park, more water slides, jumping pillow, playground, giant chess, mini golf: there's heaps for the kids to do. Facilities kept in good order too (I like the individual little bathrooms as opposed to one large amenities block).
Only downside is location. Check the map before you book, the park is 20-25 minutes from Townsville City and right on a highway, so you do get a bit of highway noise.

Great holiday park with so many activities for kids big and little!  Water slides, two pools, playgrounds, mini golf, jumping pillow… my kids loved it. Cabins were also well appointed and had everything you need including good wifi.

Camping calme et pas très loin de la ville de Townsville.
Le personnel est très sympathique.
Le prix est vraiment abordable surtout dans ce secteur géographique de l'Australie où tout est un peu plus cher qu'ailleurs.
Nous avons pris une nuit au départ et finalement nous avons pris une deuxième nuit.

Emplacement pour van
Camping avec des supers aires de jeux mais pas assez de cuisines et de sanitaires
La cuisine est pas mal
Les sanitaires ne sont pas supers

Nice cabins on a peacefull park. Cabins fully airconditioned, Hot a little kitchenette, fridge and 2 bedrooms. Also a veranda. Swimmingpool for Kids, but they have to be 120cm to fully enjoy the slides. Our kid was Just to small and we didnt know this. So, by this knowledge we had not stay here. Thats why we give service a lower rate. Location Just outside townsville.

Bit disappointed had family staying at Park so went to visit them & take advantage of the water park.
Arrived at 3.30 pm & was charged $15.00 ea for the wife & I to use the water park slides.
We walked to the park & watched the grandchildren use the slides 🛝 which we couldn't use.
The only slides 🛝 we could use closed at 4.00pm, so after watching grandchildren for a while, went to go on the big slides guess what they were closed. What I'm peeved about is that on arrival we weren't told that the would be closed in half an hour but still the took our money.
Not impressed.
Great park with awesome facilities and clean amenities. Had to get in early for a shower or the hot water ran out. Otherwise i would give it 5 stars.

This is wow for the kiddies. Splash pad, water slide plus a separate pool,  large sand area with swings, 4 wheel buggies to hire.  Reception was very organised with the paperwork.  Nearby supermarket and other shops. I didn't stay in a room so can't comment on those. I expect they would be good though based on how the park looks.
Great to be able to buy hot snacks and beers from their kiosk near the pool and water slides. Our caravan was right there near it all with a view from outside of the kids as well. Thanks.
Love the place ❤
Great caravan park

Nice enough park, but looking a bit tired in parts. Recreational facilities for kids are good (pool, jumping pillows etc) but no games room. Camp kitchen is very basic, as are the amenities. They are adequate but could certainly do with some upgrades and more regular cleaning. |Biggest issue was when it rains. Some of the roads are just dirt (not asphalt) so when it rains it become a big sloppy mud pie. The sort where you sink into it and slip and slide when you try and walk on it. This is also the case with the camp sites. Not a problem I guess if you’re in a caravan, but if in a tent then you won’t be happy campers!!  Other sites we have stayed at have fake turf or shade cloth material attached to the ground to give a good solid base. That would’ve been helpful here. Also if you’re in a tent the birds will wake you before the sun is up, and possibly throughout the night. It’s also a  bit of a drive out of the main town so I think if I was to return to Townsville, I would stay somewhere closer to town.

Nice grassy sites.
Well laid out.
Staff were brilliant
Lots of space to walk our dogs.
Shame about the cold weather
20 mins from The Strand.

Came in at 4.10pm but they were closed,  so we couldn’t get an unpowered site for the night. Was a pity as we had friends in there, and the park looked nice. In my opinion, 4pm is very early for a place like this to shut for the day!
Very convenient location with good amenities and great pool/slide area. Food trucks as a win too

If I could give a 0 I would. You had the option of pay now or later which I did to pay there and when we ring when it's end of checking you tell US THAT YOU SOLD THE ROOM ON US. WILL NOT RECOMMEND YOU TO ANYONE
Clean and tidy park and very friendly and helpful staff. Pet friendly.

Large grassy sites. Friendly staff and easy check in. Quiet caravan park. So much for the kids to do. Awesome waterslides, mini golf, watepark and jumpy pillow. Clean facilities. Only stayed 2 nights but should have stayed more. We will be back.
Way overpriced.

Place is looking a bit tired but we could forgive that. However the jumping pillow was very under inflated, so much so that kids hit the firm ground underneath. We told the reception staff member as thought it was a bit of a hazard for the public. All this got us was a whole lot of ATTITUDE and RUDENESS from the female reception staff member. You should change jobs!!. Customer service is not your cup of tea. Maybe night packing at Woollies where you can take your frustrations out on a can of baked beans is for you! We’ve stayed at Ingenia Rivershore, Cairns Coconut, Tewantin, and more of your parks. You guys need to up your game.

Lacking some of the services that Cairns has. Rooms need work but are ok.
Would expect to pay less than cairns considering the place looks old.

We have been scammed and we received emails saying that we paid for a visit and $944 is missing from our credit card. We then called the resort and they refused to give us a refund because it is not 24 hours away from us “staying”

My family stayed in a 2brm deluxe cabin. The unit was clean and comfortable, with three airconditioners, washing machine and dryer. Access off the highway was easy. The park was full but still quiet. Waterpark with waterslides were popular for kids.

The park was great, staff, facilities etc. Unfortunately, we got little to no sleep due to the highway, a barking dog & a rooster going off  every night. We also unfortunately had a view of a dump station, 4 skip bins & a septic pond. The smell was so horrible that we couldn't go sit out the front of our cabin without getting the huge whiff of poo coming across the cabin and making us feel ill. On a good note. The pool and facilities were amazing! We had a great time when we went out exploring the park and the staff were very helpful. Mixed feeling on leaving this review, as the park was great, just not the location of our cabin. Still recommend. Great for families with kids.

My family and I have stayed at this caravan park numerous of times.
My family and I have always had a great time. Unfortunately one of the office staff there is extremely rude, and unhelpful.
Will never 👎 stay there again after this morning experience.
Lovely park with great activities for young and old.
Great service staying 3 nights
Too far from the CBD
Great location for us, staff welcoming and polite, Coles only a few minutes drive away. Amenities were kept clean. Great park if you gave children. Would definitely stay again.🙂

Our stay at Ingenia Holidays Big4 in Townsville was fantastic! The ensuite sites were spotlessly clean, providing a comfortable and convenient camping experience. The staff delivered excellent service, ensuring our needs were met promptly. I highly recommend this caravan park for a pleasant and hassle-free holiday, and the kids especially liked the waterslides

We stayed for 2 nights with an Ensuite powered site. The ensuite was old but very clean with great water pressure. There was no cabinet for a man to shave and no floor mat (but these are minor issues). The site was neatly maintained, bins close by and roads were nice and wide for reversing a caravan.
The park has great features for children and would very popular at holiday times.
The only down side is there's a highway on each side of the caravan park and a nearby train line.
It was a nice park to visit and would recommend it to families.
